headline: Healthcare Spending at 4.8% of GDP Trails ASEAN Peers
context: Malaysia's total health expenditure as share of GDP remains below regional peers despite increasing healthcare needs.

cards.big: Healthcare spending at 4.8% of GDP in 2025 below Thailand at 5.4% and Vietnam at 5.7%
cards.sub: Lower spending correlates with longer waits fewer specialists and older equipment

cards.big: Government health spending per capita is RM2,100 or approximately USD480 in 2025
cards.sub: Amount covers only basic services forcing patients to pay out-of-pocket for gaps

cards.big: MOH requested RM42 billion for 2026 but received RM38 billion a 10% shortfall
cards.sub: Annual budget negotiations produce unpredictable funding undermining long-term planning

cards.big: WHO recommends minimum 5% of GDP for health with 6% target for universal coverage
cards.sub: Malaysia has never reached even the minimum recommended threshold for adequate health financing

cards.big: A country that spends less on health than its poorer neighbors should not be surprised by worse outcomes
Should Malaysia legislate a minimum health spending floor of 6% of GDP
